steps.title: Identify the verb needed
steps.content: The subject is “The word ‘pessimism’” and the adverb “first” appears before the blank. The sentence refers to a specific past event (a 1794 letter), so the blank must contain a finite verb in the simple past tense to agree with the subject and time reference.

steps.title: Apply the verb form rule
steps.content:
Choice B “appeared” is a simple past verb that correctly completes the clause.

The other three options are non‑finite forms (infinitive, present participle, past participle) that cannot act as the main verb of the sentence, so they break the subject‑verb structure.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
